problem_statement: With LLM 'vibe coding' producing first-draft code (here text-to-SQL) at scale, teams lack a reliable, automated way to decide which model-written queries are safe to accept without routing everything to a human reviewer.
magnitude_evidence: Modern teams ship large volumes of small changes across many services and time zones; expert reviewers are scarce and expensive, review queues create latency (qualitative, no stat)
insufficiency_evidence: single-model judges are uneven; 'use one LLM as the reviewer does not give a stable or uniformly safe operating point'; some single judges reach TPR near 1.0 but FPR above 0.6, passing many incorrect queries; 'Human review helps, but it does not scale.'
